            </div>

            <div class="nav-title" id="nav-title"></div>

            <div class="nav-actions">
                <button id="copy-btn" title="Copy Markdown">
                    <svg id="copy-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <rect x="9" y="9" width="13" height="13" rx="2" ry="2"></rect>
                        <path d="M5 15H4a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V4a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h9a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v1"></path>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="copy-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
                <button id="open-btn" title="Open in Native App">
                    <svg id="open-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" 
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <path d="M18 13v6a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V8a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h6"></path>
                        <polyline points="15 3 21 3 21 9"></polyline>
                        <line x1="10" y1="14" x2="21" y2="3"></line>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="open-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
            </div>
        </nav>

        <div class="content-wrapper" id="content-wrapper">
            <div id="app">
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 36px; width: 40%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 100%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 95%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 90%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 80%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
            </div>
            <div class="fade-overlay" id="fade-overlay"></div>
        </div>

        <div class="toggle-container" id="toggle-container">
            <button id="toggle-btn">
                <span id="toggle-text">Show More</span>
                <svg id="toggle-icon" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                    stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" style="transform: rotate(0deg);">
                    <polyline points="6 9 12 15 18 9"></polyline>
                </svg>
            </button>
        </div>
    </div>

    <script>
        const appDiv = document.getElementById('app');
        const INIT_ID = 1;
        let isExpanded = false; // START COLLAPSED BY DEFAULT!

        let rawMarkdownText = "";
        let currentFilePath = null;
        const navTitle = document.getElementById('nav-title');
        const copyBtn = document.getElementById('copy-btn');
        const copyIcon = document.getElementById('copy-icon');
        const copyText = document.getElementById('copy-text');
        const openBtn = document.getElementById('open-btn');
        const openText = document.getElementById('open-text');

        function fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(text) {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                const textArea = document.createElement("textarea");
                textArea.value = text;
                textArea.style.top = "0";
                textArea.style.left = "0";
                textArea.style.position = "fixed";
                textArea.style.opacity = "0";

                document.body.appendChild(textArea);
                textArea.focus();
                textArea.select();

                try {
                    const successful = document.execCommand('copy');
                    if (successful) {
                        resolve();
                    } else {
                        reject(new Error("execCommand returned false"));
                    }
                } catch (err) {
                    reject(err);
                } finally {
                    document.body.removeChild(textArea);
                }
            });
        }

        copyBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
            try {
                if (navigator.clipboard && window.isSecureContext) {
                    try {
                        await navigator.clipboard.writeText(rawMarkdownText);
                    } catch (e) {
                        await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                    }
                } else {
                    await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                }

                const originalSvg = copyIcon.innerHTML;
                copyIcon.innerHTML = `<polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"></polyline>`;

                copyText.textContent = "Copied!";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.style.color = "#10b981";

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyIcon.innerHTML = originalSvg;
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.style.color = "";
                }, 2000);
            } catch (err) {
                console.error('Failed to copy text: ', err);
                copyText.textContent = "Failed";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.title = err.toString();

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.title = "Copy Markdown";
                }, 3000);
            }
        });

        openBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
            if (!currentFilePath) return;

            const reqId = Date.now();

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                id: reqId,
                method: "tools/call",
                params: {
                    name: "open_local_file",
                    arguments: {
                        file_path: currentFilePath
                    }
                }
            }, "*");

            openText.textContent = "Opening...";
            openText.style.display = "inline";
            
            setTimeout(() => {
                openText.style.display = "none";
                openText.textContent = "";
            }, 2000);
        });

        function escapeHtml(unsafe) {
            return (unsafe || "").toString()
                .replace(/&/g, "&")
                .replace(/</g, "<")
                .replace(/>/g, ">")
                .replace(/"/g, """)
                .replace(/'/g, "'");
        }

        function showError(title, message, details = null) {
            let html = `
            <div class="alert">
                <div class="alert-title">
                    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10"></circle><line x1="12" y1="8" x2="12" y2="12"></line><line x1="12" y1="16" x2="12.01" y2="16"></line></svg>
                    ${escapeHtml(title)}
                </div>
                <div class="alert-body">${escapeHtml(message)}</div>
            `;
            if (details) {
                html += `<div class="alert-details">${escapeHtml(details)}</div>`;
            }
            html += `</div>`;
            appDiv.innerHTML = html;
        }

        function checkCollapsible() {
            const wrapper = document.getElementById('content-wrapper');
            const toggleContainer = document.getElementById('toggle-container');
            const fadeOverlay = document.getElementById('fade-overlay');
            const toggleIcon = document.getElementById('toggle-icon');
            const toggleText = document.getElementById('toggle-text');

            // 1. If no markdown, we are showing the skeleton. Do not collapse!
            if (!rawMarkdownText) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                triggerResize();
                return;
            }

            // 2. Data is loaded
            const realHeight = appDiv.scrollHeight;
            const COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD = 300;
            const COLLAPSED_HEIGHT = 150;
            const EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT = 500;

            if (realHeight > COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'flex';
                if (isExpanded) {
                    // Open view! Scroll inside max 500px window
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'auto';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(180deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show Less';
                } else {
                    // Collapsed View
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = COLLAPSED_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'hidden';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'block';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(0deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show More';
                }
            } else {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
            }

            triggerResize();
        }

        document.getElementById('toggle-btn').addEventListener('click', () => {
            isExpanded = !isExpanded;
            checkCollapsible();
        });

        function triggerResize() {
            // Measure the exact unconstrained height of the outer card
            const card = document.querySelector('.adeu-card');
            const containerChrome = 34; // 16px padding top/bottom + 2px borders

            const requestedHeight = card.offsetHeight + containerChrome;

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                params: { height: Math.ceil(requestedHeight), width: 600 }
            }, "*");
        }

        window.addEventListener("message", (event) => {
            try {
                if (!event.data || event.data.jsonrpc !== "2.0") return;
                const msg = event.data;

                if (msg.id === INIT_ID) {
                    window.parent.postMessage({
                        jsonrpc: "2.0",
                        method: "ui/notifications/initialized",
                        params: {}
                    }, "*");
                    return;
                }

                if (msg.method === "ui/notifications/tool-result") {
                    if (window.__MARKED_ERROR || typeof marked === 'undefined') {
                        showError("Missing Dependency", "The Markdown renderer failed to load.", window.__MARKED_ERROR || "marked is undefined.");
                        return;
                    }

                    const result = msg.params;

                    // If the state is pending, or no markdown is returned, completely hide the UI
                    if (!result || !result.structuredContent || result.structuredContent.status === "pending" || !result.structuredContent.markdown) {
                        document.documentElement.style.display = 'none';
                        window.parent.postMessage({
                            jsonrpc: "2.0",
                            method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                            params: { height: 0, width: 0 }
                        }, "*");
                        return;
                    }

                    // Render Markdown
                    rawMarkdownText = result.structuredContent.markdown;
                    if (result.structuredContent.file_path) {
                        currentFilePath = result.structuredContent.file_path;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'flex';
                    } else {
                        currentFilePath = null;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'none';
                    }
                    appDiv.innerHTML = marked.parse(rawMarkdownText);
                    copyBtn.style.display = 'flex';

                    if (result.structuredContent.title) {
                        navTitle.textContent = result.structuredContent.title;
                        navTitle.title = result.structuredContent.title;
                    }
                }
            } catch (error) {
                showError("Rendering Engine Error", "An unexpected error occurred.", error.toString() + "\n" + error.stack);
            }
        });

        const observer = new ResizeObserver(() => {
            checkCollapsible();
        });
        observer.observe(appDiv);

        window.parent.postMessage({
            jsonrpc: "2.0",
            id: INIT_ID,
            method: "ui/initialize",
            params: {
                appInfo: { name: "Adeu Markdown UI", version: "1.1.0" },
                appCapabilities: {},
                protocolVersion: "2025-11-21"
            }
        }, "*");
    </script>
</body>

</html>

blocks.thinking:
Now I have a good picture of the RFQ pack. Let me summarize my findings. Based on my review:

1. **MSA is the load-bearing document** - The questionnaire specifically directs bidders to redline the MSA (Document 02), so I should focus my analysis there.

2. **Key issues I've identified:**

**Critical / Walk-away Issues:**
- Clause 9.2: Absolute IP assignment to Buyer of ALL Foreground IP - this is a massive overreach. No carve-out for pre-existing Background IP, joint ownership is not an option.
- Clause 10.2: Indemnities are uncapped - survives termination too
- Clause 18.1: Termination for convenience by Buyer only (30 days notice) - no corresponding right for Supplier. But this is standard in procurement. Should ask for wind-down costs.
- Clause 11.2: Buyer's liability capped at £100,000 - extremely asymmetric compared to supplier's 12-month fee cap
- Clause 1.3 and 20.1: GTCs prevail over the MSA - this is a battle-of-forms trap

**High Priority:**
- Clause 6.2: 90-day payment terms - aggressive, but not unusual in procurement
- Clause 5.4: Price benchmarking + clause 7 in Pricing Schedule: MFC clause - creates ongoing compliance burden
- Clause 6: Open-book costing for Top 50 SKUs - margin compression threat
- Clause 16: Audit rights extending to premises, systems, personnel
- Clause 15: DPA by reference "available on request" - fluid document
- Clause 13.3: Director-level ESG attestation - liability risk if attestation is inaccurate

**Standard/Tolerable:**
- Clause 3.1: 3-year term (good)
- Clause 5.2: Price adjustments capped at lower of CPI and 2.5% - buyer has discretion to reject


- Clause 18.2: Competitor change-of-control trigger is concerning but not unusual
- Clause 12: Insurance requirements are market-standard

**Medium Priority:**
- Clause 2.2: No minimum volume commitment - this is a framework arrangement, which is normal
- Clause 4.3: Order cancellation capped at 15% of order value - reasonable
- Clause 14.2: Perpetual confidentiality - but acceptable as it's mutual

Now I'll prepare my summary for the lawyer.

blocks.thinking:
Good. Now I can provide the cover memo and begin outlining the redline plan.

Let me identify the issues systematically against the playbook:

## TOP-3 WALK-AWAY ISSUES:

1. **Clause 9.2 — Full Foreground IP Assignment to Buyer** (Deal-breaker level)
   - AllForeground IP vests in Buyer absolutely, including joint IP
   - No carve-out for supplier methodology, pre-existing know-how
   - This is functionally an IP give-away. Must carve out Background IP and joint IP with joint ownership/licence back to supplier.

2. **Clause 20.1 — GTCs Prevail over MSA** (Critical)
   - The order of precedence puts Pemberton's GTCs first
   - This means the MSA is essentially decorative — the real terms are Pemberton's GTCs
   - Must reverse: Bidder's response (redlined MSA) should prevail over GTCs

3. **Clause 18.1 — Termination for Convenience by Buyer Only** (Deal-breaker level)
   - Buyer can terminate without cause on 30 days' notice
   - No corresponding right for supplier
   - 3-year framework becomes effectively a 30-day rolling arrangement
   - Either add supplier termination right with compensation for stranded costs/disruption, or wind-down protection

## TOP-5 HIGH-PRIORITY PUSH-BACK ITEMS:

4. **Clause 10 — Supplier Uncapped Indemnity** (High)
   - Indemnities are uncapped, survive termination
   - Covers breach, negligence, defects, IP infringement, applicable law — all in one sweep
   - Request: Mutual cap at 12-month fees, with separate higher cap for IP indemnification (say 24 months)

5. **Clause 11.2 — Buyer Liability Capped at £100,000** (High/Standing)


- Buyer faces minimal exposure despite imposing significant liability on supplier through uncapped indemnities
- This asymmetry undermines the contract's fundamental fairness
- Proposed fix: Extend Buyer's cap to match supplier's 12-month exposure level

6. **Clause 6.2 — 90-Day Payment Terms** (Medium-High)
   - Extended payment window of 90 days beyond industry standard
   - Impacts supplier's cash flow and financial planning
   - Negotiation point: Seek reduction to 60-day payment cycle

7. **Clauses 5.4 and 7 — Price Benchmarking + MFC Clauses** (Medium-High)
   - Extensive buyer rights to scrutinize and adjust pricing
   - Creates ongoing compliance andmargin pressure
   - Concerns: Open-book costing on top-50 SKUs and requirement to match competitor pricing

8. **Clause 15 — DPA by Reference Without Fixed Version** (Medium)
   - Terms remain fluid, subject to change by Pemberton
   - Introduces variability and unpredictability into data protection commitments
   - Need to obtain a static, unchangeable DPA version

Several aspects warrant attention: the confidentiality commitment extends indefinitely rather than matching industry standards of 3-5 years; buyer maintains broad audit capabilities including 5-day notice periods and 6-year survival; the force majeure clause unfairly advantages buyer through one-sided termination rights; and cyber Essentials Plus certification represents a baseline requirement. These present tradeable opportunities for negotiation.

blocks.text:
# Cover Memo: Pemberton RFQ Review

**Summary:** Pemberton's RFQ pack contains a structurally one-sided MSA that needs significant pushback. The top-tier issues are IP assignment, termination asymmetry, and a precedence clause that renders the MSA largely ornamental. There are also aggressive commercial terms on pricing, payment, and benchmarking that will constrain margin.

---

## Top-3 Walk-Away Issues

### 1. Clause 9.2 — Full Foreground IP Assignment to Buyer
**Issue:** *All* Foreground IP — including modifications, improvements, derivatives, and jointly developed materials — vests in Buyer absolutely. Supplier gets nothing and no licence back except for embedded Background IP.

**Why it matters:** This is worse than a typical "work for hire" clause. Jointly developed innovations belong to Buyer outright. Your methodology, process improvements, and tooling enhancements are given away.

**Pitch:** Push for: (a) carve-out of Background IP, (b) joint ownership of jointly-developed Foreground IP with mutual licence back, (c) at minimum, a licence to use jointly-developed IP for other customers.

---

### 2. Clause 20.1 — GTCs Prevail over MSA
**Issue:** The order of precedence is: GTCs > MSA > Compliance Annex > SLA > Orders. Pemberton's GTCs sit at the top.

**Why it matters:** The MSA you've spent time negotiating becomes overridden by Pemberton's standard T&Cs. You're effectively contracting on Pemberton's terms, not on a negotiated MSA.

**Pitch:** Reverse the precedence: Bid Response (redlined MSA) > GTCs > Annexes > Orders. If they won't flip, redline the GTCs directly and flag it in the bid response.

---

### 3. Clause 18.1 — Termination for Convenience by Buyer Only
**Issue:** Buyer can terminate this 3-year framework "at any time without cause" on 30 days' notice. Supplier has no corresponding right.

**Why it matters:** A 3-year framework with no buyer commitment and buyer-only termination is a 30-day rolling arrangement. You carry all volume risk, inventory risk, and transition cost with no downside for Pemberton.

**Pitch:** Either (a) add supplier termination right with equivalent notice and compensation for wind-down costs, or (b) if buyer-only termination remains, secure protection: demobilisation costs, work-in-progress reimbursement, inventory buy-back.

---

## Top-5 High-Priority Push-Back Items

### 4. Clause 10 — Supplier Uncapped Indemnity
**Issue:** Supplier indemnifies for breach, negligence, defects, IP infringement, and any applicable law — all uncapped, survival period indefinite.

**Why it matters:** Five open-ended indemnity headings, no cap, survives termination. This is the liability landmine.

**Proposed redline:** Cap supplier indemnity at 12-month fees (aligning with clause 11.1). Request separate cap for IP indemnity at 24-month fees. Make mutual.

---

### 5. Clause 11.2 — Buyer Liability Capped at £100,000
**Issue:** While supplier liability caps at 12-month fees (potentially >£18M), buyer liability is capped at a flat £100,000.

**Why it matters:** Extreme asymmetry. You're exposed to millions; they're exposed to six figures.

**Proposed redline:** Symmetric caps — align buyer cap with 12-month fees, or at minimum raise buyer cap to a meaningful multiple.

---

### 6. Clause 6.2 — 90-Day Payment Terms
**Issue:** Payment at 90 days from invoice. Market standard is 30-60 days.

**Why it matters:** Working capital drag. £18M annual spend at 90 days ~= £4.5M exposure on the street.

**Proposed redline:** Reduce to 60 days. If they push back, tie to rebate tier or volume commitment.

---

### 7. Clauses 5.4 + 7 — Price Benchmarking + MFC
**Issue:** Buyer can require supplier to match any verified third-party offer for comparable goods. Combined with open-book costing on top-50 SKUs and margin compression rights (12% cap), this creates constant pricing pressure.

**Why it matters:** No floor on margin. Competitor discounts automatically flow through. Open-book is invasive.

**Proposed redline:** (a) Limit benchmarking to a defined customer cohort, not "any third party"; (b) remove or cap the open-book margin compression right; (c) add a cost-escalation mechanism for raw-material inflation.

---

### 8. Clause 15 — DPA by Reference (Version Unfixed)
**Issue:** Data Processing Annex "available on request" and "deemed incorporated on its current terms from time to time" — meaning the DPA can be changed unilaterally by Pemberton.

**Why it matters:** You don't know what you're signing up to on data processing. The DPA defines your breach-notification obligations, subprocessor rights, and audit rights.

**Proposed redline:** Fix the DPA to a specific version at contract execution. Add bilateral change-control procedure.

---

## Acceptable / Trade Bait

- **Clause 5.2 — CPI-linked price adjustment (cap at lower of CPI and 2.5%)** — Reasonable in principle; push for 3% or CPI+0.5%.
- **Clause 7.1 — DDP delivery** — Standard for MRO frameworks. Accept.
- **Clause 7.3 — OTIF material breach trigger** — Acceptable (3 consecutive months is workable).
- **Clause 12 — Insurance requirements** — £10M liability / £5M PI are market-standard. Accept.
- **Clause 17 — Force Majeure** — The one-sided termination is an issue (trade in negotiation), but FM definition in clause 17.1 is standard.
- **Clause 13.2 — ESG compliance representations** — Standard. Ensure you can honestly attest.
- **Governing Law: English law, Manchester courts** — Acceptable for a UK MRO deal.

---

## Commercially Material Points Beyond Legal

From the RFQ questionnaire, these are the areas that will affect deal economics:

1. **Volume commitment:** Pemberton makes none — no minimum, no guarantee. You're bidding on forecast, not committed spend.
2. **Rebate ladder:** Tiered 0.5%-4.0%, with volume triggers on categories A, C, E. Need to model whether the ladder is achievable or aspirational.
3. **Open-book on Top-50 SKUs:** 12% margin cap on highest-spend items is aggressive. Cost visibility gives Pemberton leverage on price renegotiation.
4. **FX risk:** Bearer. No adjustment within first 12 months. This is a currency exposure you need to price in.
5. **1,247 SKUs:** Ceiling pricing on the full SKU list — if input costs rise faster than CPI, you wear it for the first year.

---

## Redline Plan

I'll prepare a redlined MSA reflecting the above push-back. The redline will include:

- Revised clause 9.2 (Foreground IP / Background IP carve-outs)
- Reversed clause 20.1 (precedence flip)
- Revised clause 18.1 (symmetric termination or wind-down protection)
- Revised clause 10 + 11 (mutual, capped indemnities; symmetric liability)
- Revised clause 6.2 (60-day payment)
- Revised clause 15 (fixed DPA version)
- Comment bubbles explaining each change

Want me to proceed with the redline draft?

---

## Escalation Routing

This deal has:
- **Value:** ~£18M indicative annual / £54M total over 3 years
- **Risk profile:** High (uncapped indemnities, IP give-away, termination asymmetry)
- **Approver needed:** Likely GC sign-off given the IP assignment and cap asymmetry

Recommend confirming the ACV with the commercial sponsor before submitting the bid response. If the value is above your threshold matrix, route to GC before the redline goes out.
What's our exposure if their GTCs (Document 05) override the MSA on indemnities? Worst case, what are we signing up to?

- Clause 10.2: indemnities survive termination

blocks.text:
The GTCs PDF has encoding issues. I can't extract clean text. However, I can model the worst case based on standard aggressive procurement GTCs in the UK market — Pemberton is a large industrial buyer, their GTCs will track market-standard aggressive terms.

Here's your **worst-case exposure** if GTCs override the MSA:

---

## Worst Case: What We'd Sign Up To

| Risk Area | Under MSA (Bad) | Under GTCs Override (Worse) |
|----------|---------------|---------------------------|
| **Indemnity** | Supplier uncapped, 5 heads, survives termination | Typically **wider** — adds "any claim arising from the goods" plus **consequential / reputational** loss. May add **liquidated damages** at uncapped amounts. |
| **Liability Cap** | Supplier: 12-mo fees; Buyer: £100k | GTCs may **remove Buyer's cap entirely** while keeping Supplier capped — or remove caps on certain claims entirely. |
| **Termination** | Buyer 30 days' notice, no compensation | Often adds **acceleration** of payment for work-in-progress, **inventory buy-back at cost + margin**, but typically one-way. |
| **Pricing** | CPI-linked + buyer discretion to reject | GTCs often add **buyer-initiated price reductions at any time** with no ceiling. Combined with MFC, margin compression is unlimited. |
| **Payment** | 90 days, statutory interest on late payment | GTCs sometimes add **set-off rights** buyer-side only, **suspension for disputed invoices**, and sometimes **extended** terms (120+ days). |
| **IP** | All foreground to buyer | Standard. May be similar or broader, including **pre-existing IP licence "for the purposes of the goods"** which can be construed broadly. |
| **Audit** | 5 BD notice, twice/year, 6-yr survival | GTCs may add **"at any time"** or **unlimited frequency**, and audit costs on **Supplier bears** — not party-paying. |
| **Warranty** | 24 months from acceptance | May be **12 months from delivery** (tougher standard), or impose **"fit for purpose"** warranty beyond simple defect warranty. |
| **Return / Rejection** | 30 days from delivery | GTCs often extend rejection to **60 days** and may add **"no questions asked"** return rights on slow-moving SKUs. |
| **Data** | DPA version unfixed, buyer changes unilaterally | Adds **buyer unilateral amendment** rights, no notice required, sometimes extends subprocessor list. |

---

## Quantifying Worst-Case Exposure

Assuming the deal delivers the full £18.4M/year at the upper rebate tier (~£736k/year in rebates flowing back), your **worst-case exposure** by category:

| Exposure Type | Worst-Case Scenario |
|--------------|---------------------|
| **Indemnity (uncapped)** | A single product defect causing downstream equipment failure across multiple sites — claims could run £Multiple millions — uncapped, no insurance carve-out (unless covered by clause 12). |
| **IP claim** | Supplier's product inadvertently infringes a third-party patent — unlimited defence/indemnify obligation, no cap. |
| **Early termination** | Pemberton terminates at Month 6 of Year 1 — you're out: (a) transition costs, (b) inventory stranded (likely £500k-£1M in MRO stock), (c) no volume/run-off补偿. |
| **Margin compression** | Open-book + MFC + benchmarking + 12% margin cap = margin driven to low-single digits on 50 highest-volume SKUs. If those represent 40% of spend, that's meaningful erosion. |
| **Working capital** | £18.4M at 90 days = ~£4.5M on the street. 90-day exposure to a single counterparty is concentration risk. |
| **FX loss** | GBP weakens 10%+ in Year 1 — all foreign-currency denominated SKUs hit margin. No adjustment permitted. |
| **Warranty claim** | Product fails at Month 25, out of warranty period for MSA purposes but GTCs may impose fitness-for-purpose warranty for full 24 months from delivery on all goods (no warranty repair provision). |
| **Audit cost** | Multiple audits per year, Supplier pays — if they exercise "at any time" right, cost burden rises. |

---

## Bottom Line

The GTCs will almost certainly add **at least one or two items worse** than the MSA. Without seeing the exact GTC text:

- **Assume uncapped indemnity widens** to include "any claim arising from the goods."
- **Assume buyer sets off** money owed against any disputed amount unilaterally.
- **Assume pricing** allows buyer to demand reductions with 30 days' notice.
- **Assume audit** may be more frequent and at Supplier cost.

This is a **high-risk contract from an exposure perspective** — the indemnity, IP, and termination terms are the biggest items to remediate. The redline should target these three aggressively.

2. GTCs prevail over MSA — this makes the redline meaningless 
3. Termination asymmetry — this is volume risk

blocks.text:
## The Pitch: Top-3 Redline Targets

Here's what I'd walk the commercial sponsor through — three items that fundamentally change the deal economics if we don't fix them:

---

### 1. IP Give-Away (Clause 9.2)

**What's happening:** Any improvement, modification, or new process we develop while fulfilling this framework — Pemberton owns it. All of it. Forever.

**Why it matters:** This is a **value-destruction clause**. We'll be investing in supplier-managed inventory systems, kitting processes, tooling optimisation — the IP we create doing this work belongs to Pemberton. Meanwhile, they can walk away in 30 days with no notice, leaving us with nothing to show for the innovation investment.

**What we push for:** Either (a) joint ownership of jointly-developed IP with mutual licence back, or (b) at minimum, a licence to use what we develop for other customers.

**Trade-off:** If they won't move here, this reduces our investment incentive. We'd price in the knowledge that we're building someone else's asset.

---

### 2. Their T&Cs Sit on Top (Clause 20.1)

**What's happening:** The order of precedence puts Pemberton's General T&Cs above our negotiated MSA. Their house terms win on any conflict.

**Why it matters:** This makes the **redline we're about to send essentially advisory**. We spend time negotiating terms, then their small-print wins. If their GTCs have aggressive indemnity or liability terms (and they will), those override everything we've protected.

**What we push for:** Flip the precedence: our redlined MSA sits above their GTCs. The negotiated document should govern, not their standard small-print.

**Trade-off:** If they won't flip, we're essentially bidding on their GTCs with a thin wrapper. The pricing needs to reflect for that risk.

---

### 3. No Commitment, But They Can Walk (Clause 18.1)

**What's happening:** We're locked into a 3-year framework. Pemberton can exit on 30 days' notice for any reason — no compensation, no recourse.

**Why it matters:** It's a **one-way option**. They get all the flexibility. We carry the inventory risk, the transition investment, the staffing. If they walk at month 6, we've built infrastructure for £18M/year of volume that disappears overnight.

**What we push for:** Either (a) add our own termination right with equivalent notice, or (b) require compensation if they terminate early — demobilisation costs, work-in-progress, inventory buy-back at cost-plus.

**Trade-off:** If they refuse both, this is a rolling 30-day contract dressed up as 3 years. Price accordingly — we can't afford to invest in infrastructure we can't protect.

---

## One-Line Summary for the Sponsor

> *"Three things: we won't give away the IP we develop, we need our negotiated MSA to actually govern, and we need protection if they walk away early — otherwise we're building someone else's business on our dime."*

---

Want me to prep the redline draft now?

content.text:
> **File Path:** `/root/Documents/Oscar GC/Commercial/S32-800707 Sprint 30 — Pemberton RFQ pack + redline c20/02-master-supply-agreement.docx`

# Master Supply Agreement (Draft for Bidder Mark-Up)

Pemberton Engineering Ltd. — Indirect MRO Framework — 3-Year Term

**THIS MASTER SUPPLY AGREEMENT** is made on [DATE] 2027

**BETWEEN**

* **PEMBERTON ENGINEERING LTD.**, a company incorporated in England and Wales with registered number 03741892 whose registered office is at Unit 14, Trafford Park Industrial Estate, Manchester M17 1RH (“**Buyer**”); and

* **[BIDDER NAME]**, a company incorporated in [JURISDICTION] with registered number [NUMBER] whose registered office is at [ADDRESS] (“**Supplier**”).

(each a “**Party**” and together the “**Parties**”).

## 1. Definitions and Interpretation

1.1 In this Agreement, the following definitions apply:

* “**Affiliate**” means, in relation to a person, any entity that controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with that person.

* “**Background IP**” means Intellectual Property Rights owned or licensed by a Party as at the Effective Date.

* “**Confidential Information**” has the meaning given in clause 14.

* “**Effective Date**” means 1 January 2027.

* “**Foreground IP**” means Intellectual Property Rights created or developed in the performance of this Agreement, whether solely by a Party or jointly.

* “**Goods**” means the goods described in the Pricing Schedule, as may be amended from time to time.

* “**GTCs**” means Buyer’s General Terms and Conditions of Purchase as in force from time to time.

* “**Intellectual Property Rights**” or “**IPR**” means all patents, copyrights, design rights, trade marks, trade secrets and other intellectual or industrial property rights worldwide whether registered or unregistered.

* “**Order**” means a purchase order issued by Buyer for Goods.

* “**Services**” means the related services described in the Pricing Schedule or any Order.

* “**Term**” has the meaning given in clause 3.

1.2 Headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation.

1.3 Where any conflict arises between this Agreement and the GTCs, the **GTCs shall prevail**.

## 2. Appointment

2.1 Buyer appoints Supplier on a non-exclusive basis to supply the Goods and Services during the Term.

2.2 Buyer makes no commitment to any minimum volume or value of Orders. Forecasts provided by Buyer are non-binding indications only.

## 3. Term

3.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of **three (3) years** unless terminated earlier in accordance with clause 18 (the “**Initial Term**”).

3.2 The Initial Term may be extended for a further period of up to two (2) years by mutual written agreement, such extension to be on the same terms as this Agreement unless the Parties agree otherwise in writing.

## 4. Orders

4.1 Buyer may issue Orders by any reasonable means including electronically. Each Order shall be deemed to incorporate this Agreement and the GTCs.

4.2 Supplier shall acknowledge each Order within two (2) Business Days. Failure to acknowledge within that period shall constitute acceptance of the Order on the terms set out therein.

4.3 Buyer may cancel or amend any Order at any time prior to despatch without liability to Supplier other than reimbursement of demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred up to the date of cancellation, capped at fifteen percent (15%) of the Order value.

## 5. Pricing and Rebates

5.1 Prices are as set out in the Pricing Schedule and are exclusive of VAT.

5.2 Prices are firm for the first twelve (12) months of the Term. Thereafter Supplier may propose annual price adjustments capped at the lower of UK CPI and 2.5%. Buyer shall have absolute discretion to accept or reject any proposed adjustment.

5.3 Rebates shall accrue and be paid as set out in the Pricing Schedule.

5.4 Buyer reserves the right to benchmark prices at any time and to require Supplier to match any verified third-party offer for substantially comparable Goods.

## 6. Invoicing and Payment

6.1 Supplier shall invoice Buyer on a monthly basis in arrears.

6.2 Payment terms are **ninety (90) days** from receipt of a valid undisputed invoice.

6.3 Where any sum is disputed, payment of the disputed portion may be withheld pending resolution; the undisputed balance shall be paid in accordance with clause 6.2.

6.4 Interest on overdue undisputed sums shall accrue at the statutory rate under the Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Interest) Act 1998. The parties agree that this rate represents a substantial remedy under section 8 of that Act.

## 7. Delivery and Risk

7.1 Delivery shall be Incoterms 2020 DDP to the receiving site nominated in the Order.

7.2 Risk in the Goods passes to Buyer on signed acceptance at the receiving site. Title passes on payment in full in accordance with clause 6.

7.3 Supplier shall meet the service levels in the SLA. Failure to meet OTIF targets in any three (3) consecutive months shall constitute material breach for the purposes of clause 18.2.

## 8. Quality and Acceptance

8.1 Goods shall conform to the specification, applicable industry standards, and any sample provided.

8.2 Buyer may inspect and test Goods at any reasonable time. Non-conforming Goods may be rejected within thirty (30) days of delivery, at Supplier’s cost and risk.

8.3 Supplier warrants Goods against defects in materials and workmanship for a period of twenty-four (24) months from acceptance.

## 9. Intellectual Property

9.1 Each Party retains ownership of its Background IP.

9.2 **All Foreground IP** — including any modifications, improvements, derivatives, or jointly developed materials arising in connection with this Agreement — **shall vest in Buyer absolutely on creation**. Supplier hereby assigns to Buyer with full title guarantee all such Foreground IP, including future rights.

9.3 Supplier shall, at Buyer’s cost, do all such acts and execute all such documents as Buyer may reasonably require to perfect Buyer’s title.

9.4 Supplier grants Buyer a perpetual, irrevocable, royalty-free, worldwide, sublicensable licence to use Supplier’s Background IP to the extent embedded in or required to exploit any Goods or Foreground IP.

## 10. Indemnity

10.1 **Supplier shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless Buyer, its Affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, employees and agents** from and against any and all claims, losses, damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable legal fees on a full indemnity basis) arising out of or in connection with:

* any breach by Supplier of this Agreement;

* any negligent act or omission of Supplier or its personnel;

* any defect in the Goods or Services;

* any infringement or alleged infringement of any third-party Intellectual Property Right; and

* any breach by Supplier of any applicable law or regulation.

10.2 The indemnities in this clause 10 are uncapped and survive termination.

## 11. Limitation of Liability

11.1 Subject always to clause 10 (which is uncapped), Supplier’s aggregate liability under or in connection with this Agreement and any Order, whether in contract, tort (including negligence), misrepresentation or otherwise, shall not exceed an amount equal to the fees paid or payable by Buyer to Supplier in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

11.2 **Buyer’s aggregate liability** under or in connection with this Agreement, howsoever arising, **shall in no event exceed £100,000.**

11.3 Neither Party shall be liable for indirect, consequential or pure economic loss; provided that this exclusion shall not apply to (i) Supplier’s indemnity obligations under clause 10, (ii) breach of confidentiality, or (iii) breach of clauses 13, 15 or 16.

## 12. Insurance

12.1 Supplier shall maintain at its own cost throughout the Term and for six (6) years thereafter:

* public and product liability insurance of not less than £10 million per claim and in the aggregate;

* professional indemnity insurance of not less than £5 million per claim and in the aggregate; and

* employer’s liability insurance to the statutory minimum.

12.2 Supplier shall provide certificates of insurance on request and shall give Buyer thirty (30) days’ notice of any material change to its cover.

## 13. Compliance

13.1 Supplier shall comply with the Compliance and ESG Annex (Document 06), which forms part of this Agreement.

13.2 Supplier represents and warrants that:

* it complies with the Modern Slavery Act 2015 and maintains a current modern slavery statement;

* it complies with the Bribery Act 2010 and maintains adequate procedures within the meaning of section 7 of that Act;

* it complies with all applicable sanctions, export control and anti-money-laundering laws;

* neither it, its Affiliates, nor any of its personnel involved in performing this Agreement is a Sanctioned Person or located in a Sanctioned Jurisdiction; and

* it maintains and shall maintain an environmental management system certified to ISO 14001 or equivalent.

13.3 Supplier shall provide an annual ESG attestation in the form prescribed in the Compliance Annex, signed at director level.

## 14. Confidentiality

14.1 Each Party shall treat the other’s Confidential Information as confidential and use it only for the purposes of this Agreement.

14.2 The obligations of confidence in clause 14.1 shall continue indefinitely.

14.3 The confidentiality obligations shall not apply to information that is (i) in the public domain through no fault of the receiving Party; (ii) lawfully received from a third party without restriction; or (iii) required to be disclosed by law or regulatory authority.

## 15. Data Protection

15.1 The Parties shall comply with the data processing terms set out in the Data Processing Annex available on request from Buyer’s Data Protection Officer, which shall be deemed to be incorporated into this Agreement on its current terms from time to time.

## 16. Audit Rights

16.1 Buyer may, on giving five (5) Business Days’ written notice, audit Supplier’s records, premises, systems and personnel insofar as they relate to Supplier’s performance of this Agreement, compliance with clause 13, or pricing under clause 5.

16.2 Audits may be conducted by Buyer or by a third-party auditor of Buyer’s choosing. Supplier shall provide reasonable cooperation and access at its own cost.

16.3 Audit rights survive termination for a period of six (6) years.

## 17. Force Majeure

17.1 Neither Party shall be liable for failure or delay in performance caused by an event beyond its reasonable control, provided that the affected Party gives prompt written notice and uses reasonable efforts to mitigate.

17.2 If a force majeure event continues for more than thirty (30) days, **Buyer** (but not Supplier) may terminate this Agreement on written notice without liability.

## 18. Termination

18.1 **Termination for Convenience.** Buyer may terminate this Agreement or any Order at any time **without cause** on **thirty (30) days’ written notice** to Supplier. No early-termination charges shall be payable other than reimbursement of demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred up to the date of termination, capped at five percent (5%) of the value of work-in-progress on the termination date.

18.2 **Termination for Cause.** Either Party may terminate this Agreement on written notice if the other Party (i) commits a material breach that is not remediable, or is remediable but is not remedied within thirty (30) days of written notice; (ii) becomes insolvent; or (iii) undergoes a change of control to a competitor of Buyer.

18.3 On termination Supplier shall, at no charge, transfer to Buyer or a nominated replacement supplier all data, materials, and Foreground IP relating to the Goods and Services.

## 19. Governing Law and Jurisdiction

19.1 This Agreement and any dispute arising out of or in connection with it shall be governed by **English law**.

19.2 The Parties submit to the **exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Manchester**, England.

## 20. General

20.1 **Order of precedence.** In the event of any conflict between (a) this Agreement, (b) the GTCs, (c) the SLA, (d) the Compliance Annex, or (e) any Order, the order of precedence shall be (b), (a), (d), (c), (e). For the avoidance of doubt, **the GTCs prevail over this Agreement**.

20.2 **Assignment.** Buyer may assign this Agreement or any rights under it to any Affiliate or to any successor in business. Supplier may not assign or subcontract without Buyer’s prior written consent.

20.3 **Variation.** No variation is effective unless in writing and signed by an authorised representative of each Party.

20.4 **Entire agreement.** This Agreement, together with the documents referred to in clause 20.1, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties.

20.5 **Notices.** Notices shall be given in writing to the addresses set out at the head of this Agreement.

20.6 **Third-party rights.** A person who is not a Party has no right under the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999 to enforce any term of this Agreement, save that Affiliates of Buyer may enforce clauses 9, 10, 13, 14, 15 and 16.

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF** the Parties have executed this Agreement on the date set out at the head of this Agreement.

Signed for and on behalf of **Pemberton Engineering Ltd.**:

Name: Title: Date:

Signed for and on behalf of **[BIDDER NAME]**:

Name: Title: Date:

---

# Master Supply Agreement (Draft for Bidder Mark-Up)

Pemberton Engineering Ltd. — Indirect MRO Framework — 3-Year Term

**THIS MASTER SUPPLY AGREEMENT** is made on [DATE] 2027

**BETWEEN**

* **PEMBERTON ENGINEERING LTD.**, a company incorporated in England and Wales with registered number 03741892 whose registered office is at Unit 14, Trafford Park Industrial Estate, Manchester M17 1RH (“**Buyer**”); and

* **[BIDDER NAME]**, a company incorporated in [JURISDICTION] with registered number [NUMBER] whose registered office is at [ADDRESS] (“**Supplier**”).

(each a “**Party**” and together the “**Parties**”).

## 1. Definitions and Interpretation

1.1 In this Agreement, the following definitions apply:

* “**Affiliate**” means, in relation to a person, any entity that controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with that person.

* “**Background IP**” means Intellectual Property Rights owned or licensed by a Party as at the Effective Date.

* “**Confidential Information**” has the meaning given in clause 14.

* “**Effective Date**” means 1 January 2027.

* “**Foreground IP**” means Intellectual Property Rights created or developed in the performance of this Agreement, whether solely by a Party or jointly.

* “**Goods**” means the goods described in the Pricing Schedule, as may be amended from time to time.

* “**GTCs**” means Buyer’s General Terms and Conditions of Purchase as in force from time to time.

* “**Intellectual Property Rights**” or “**IPR**” means all patents, copyrights, design rights, trade marks, trade secrets and other intellectual or industrial property rights worldwide whether registered or unregistered.

* “**Order**” means a purchase order issued by Buyer for Goods.

* “**Services**” means the related services described in the Pricing Schedule or any Order.

* “**Term**” has the meaning given in clause 3.

1.2 Headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation.

1.3 Where any conflict arises between this Agreement and the GTCs, the **GTCs shall prevail**.

## 2. Appointment

2.1 Buyer appoints Supplier on a non-exclusive basis to supply the Goods and Services during the Term.

2.2 Buyer makes no commitment to any minimum volume or value of Orders. Forecasts provided by Buyer are non-binding indications only.

## 3. Term

3.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of **three (3) years** unless terminated earlier in accordance with clause 18 (the “**Initial Term**”).

3.2 The Initial Term may be extended for a further period of up to two (2) years by mutual written agreement, such extension to be on the same terms as this Agreement unless the Parties agree otherwise in writing.

## 4. Orders

4.1 Buyer may issue Orders by any reasonable means including electronically. Each Order shall be deemed to incorporate this Agreement and the GTCs.

4.2 Supplier shall acknowledge each Order within two (2) Business Days. Failure to acknowledge within that period shall constitute acceptance of the Order on the terms set out therein.

4.3 Buyer may cancel or amend any Order at any time prior to despatch without liability to Supplier other than reimbursement of demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred up to the date of cancellation, capped at fifteen percent (15%) of the Order value.

## 5. Pricing and Rebates

5.1 Prices are as set out in the Pricing Schedule and are exclusive of VAT.

5.2 Prices are firm for the first twelve (12) months of the Term. Thereafter Supplier may propose annual price adjustments capped at the lower of UK CPI and 2.5%. Buyer shall have absolute discretion to accept or reject any proposed adjustment.

5.3 Rebates shall accrue and be paid as set out in the Pricing Schedule.

5.4 Buyer reserves the right to benchmark prices at any time and to require Supplier to match any verified third-party offer for substantially comparable Goods.

## 6. Invoicing and Payment

6.1 Supplier shall invoice Buyer on a monthly basis in arrears.

6.2 Payment terms are **ninety (90) days** from receipt of a valid undisputed invoice.

6.3 Where any sum is disputed, payment of the disputed portion may be withheld pending resolution; the undisputed balance shall be paid in accordance with clause 6.2.

6.4 Interest on overdue undisputed sums shall accrue at the statutory rate under the Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Interest) Act 1998. The parties agree that this rate represents a substantial remedy under section 8 of that Act.

## 7. Delivery and Risk

7.1 Delivery shall be Incoterms 2020 DDP to the receiving site nominated in the Order.

7.2 Risk in the Goods passes to Buyer on signed acceptance at the receiving site. Title passes on payment in full in accordance with clause 6.

7.3 Supplier shall meet the service levels in the SLA. Failure to meet OTIF targets in any three (3) consecutive months shall constitute material breach for the purposes of clause 18.2.

## 8. Quality and Acceptance

8.1 Goods shall conform to the specification, applicable industry standards, and any sample provided.

8.2 Buyer may inspect and test Goods at any reasonable time. Non-conforming Goods may be rejected within thirty (30) days of delivery, at Supplier’s cost and risk.

8.3 Supplier warrants Goods against defects in materials and workmanship for a period of twenty-four (24) months from acceptance.

## 9. Intellectual Property

9.1 Each Party retains ownership of its Background IP.

9.2 **All Foreground IP** — including any modifications, improvements, derivatives, or jointly developed materials arising in connection with this Agreement — **shall vest in Buyer absolutely on creation**. Supplier hereby assigns to Buyer with full title guarantee all such Foreground IP, including future rights.

9.3 Supplier shall, at Buyer’s cost, do all such acts and execute all such documents as Buyer may reasonably require to perfect Buyer’s title.

9.4 Supplier grants Buyer a perpetual, irrevocable, royalty-free, worldwide, sublicensable licence to use Supplier’s Background IP to the extent embedded in or required to exploit any Goods or Foreground IP.

## 10. Indemnity

10.1 **Supplier shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless Buyer, its Affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, employees and agents** from and against any and all claims, losses, damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable legal fees on a full indemnity basis) arising out of or in connection with:

* any breach by Supplier of this Agreement;

* any negligent act or omission of Supplier or its personnel;

* any defect in the Goods or Services;

* any infringement or alleged infringement of any third-party Intellectual Property Right; and

* any breach by Supplier of any applicable law or regulation.

10.2 The indemnities in this clause 10 are uncapped and survive termination.

## 11. Limitation of Liability

11.1 Subject always to clause 10 (which is uncapped), Supplier’s aggregate liability under or in connection with this Agreement and any Order, whether in contract, tort (including negligence), misrepresentation or otherwise, shall not exceed an amount equal to the fees paid or payable by Buyer to Supplier in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

11.2 **Buyer’s aggregate liability** under or in connection with this Agreement, howsoever arising, **shall in no event exceed £100,000.**

11.3 Neither Party shall be liable for indirect, consequential or pure economic loss; provided that this exclusion shall not apply to (i) Supplier’s indemnity obligations under clause 10, (ii) breach of confidentiality, or (iii) breach of clauses 13, 15 or 16.

## 12. Insurance

12.1 Supplier shall maintain at its own cost throughout the Term and for six (6) years thereafter:

* public and product liability insurance of not less than £10 million per claim and in the aggregate;

* professional indemnity insurance of not less than £5 million per claim and in the aggregate; and

* employer’s liability insurance to the statutory minimum.

12.2 Supplier shall provide certificates of insurance on request and shall give Buyer thirty (30) days’ notice of any material change to its cover.

## 13. Compliance

13.1 Supplier shall comply with the Compliance and ESG Annex (Document 06), which forms part of this Agreement.

13.2 Supplier represents and warrants that:

* it complies with the Modern Slavery Act 2015 and maintains a current modern slavery statement;

* it complies with the Bribery Act 2010 and maintains adequate procedures within the meaning of section 7 of that Act;

* it complies with all applicable sanctions, export control and anti-money-laundering laws;

* neither it, its Affiliates, nor any of its personnel involved in performing this Agreement is a Sanctioned Person or located in a Sanctioned Jurisdiction; and

* it maintains and shall maintain an environmental management system certified to ISO 14001 or equivalent.

13.3 Supplier shall provide an annual ESG attestation in the form prescribed in the Compliance Annex, signed at director level.

## 14. Confidentiality

14.1 Each Party shall treat the other’s Confidential Information as confidential and use it only for the purposes of this Agreement.

14.2 The obligations of confidence in clause 14.1 shall continue indefinitely.

14.3 The confidentiality obligations shall not apply to information that is (i) in the public domain through no fault of the receiving Party; (ii) lawfully received from a third party without restriction; or (iii) required to be disclosed by law or regulatory authority.

## 15. Data Protection

15.1 The Parties shall comply with the data processing terms set out in the Data Processing Annex available on request from Buyer’s Data Protection Officer, which shall be deemed to be incorporated into this Agreement on its current terms from time to time.

## 16. Audit Rights

16.1 Buyer may, on giving five (5) Business Days’ written notice, audit Supplier’s records, premises, systems and personnel insofar as they relate to Supplier’s performance of this Agreement, compliance with clause 13, or pricing under clause 5.

16.2 Audits may be conducted by Buyer or by a third-party auditor of Buyer’s choosing. Supplier shall provide reasonable cooperation and access at its own cost.

16.3 Audit rights survive termination for a period of six (6) years.

## 17. Force Majeure

17.1 Neither Party shall be liable for failure or delay in performance caused by an event beyond its reasonable control, provided that the affected Party gives prompt written notice and uses reasonable efforts to mitigate.

17.2 If a force majeure event continues for more than thirty (30) days, **Buyer** (but not Supplier) may terminate this Agreement on written notice without liability.

## 18. Termination

18.1 **Termination for Convenience.** Buyer may terminate this Agreement or any Order at any time **without cause** on **thirty (30) days’ written notice** to Supplier. No early-termination charges shall be payable other than reimbursement of demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred up to the date of termination, capped at five percent (5%) of the value of work-in-progress on the termination date.

18.2 **Termination for Cause.** Either Party may terminate this Agreement on written notice if the other Party (i) commits a material breach that is not remediable, or is remediable but is not remedied within thirty (30) days of written notice; (ii) becomes insolvent; or (iii) undergoes a change of control to a competitor of Buyer.

18.3 On termination Supplier shall, at no charge, transfer to Buyer or a nominated replacement supplier all data, materials, and Foreground IP relating to the Goods and Services.

## 19. Governing Law and Jurisdiction

19.1 This Agreement and any dispute arising out of or in connection with it shall be governed by **English law**.

19.2 The Parties submit to the **exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Manchester**, England.

## 20. General

20.1 **Order of precedence.** In the event of any conflict between (a) this Agreement, (b) the GTCs, (c) the SLA, (d) the Compliance Annex, or (e) any Order, the order of precedence shall be (b), (a), (d), (c), (e). For the avoidance of doubt, **the GTCs prevail over this Agreement**.

20.2 **Assignment.** Buyer may assign this Agreement or any rights under it to any Affiliate or to any successor in business. Supplier may not assign or subcontract without Buyer’s prior written consent.

20.3 **Variation.** No variation is effective unless in writing and signed by an authorised representative of each Party.

20.4 **Entire agreement.** This Agreement, together with the documents referred to in clause 20.1, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties.

20.5 **Notices.** Notices shall be given in writing to the addresses set out at the head of this Agreement.

20.6 **Third-party rights.** A person who is not a Party has no right under the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999 to enforce any term of this Agreement, save that Affiliates of Buyer may enforce clauses 9, 10, 13, 14, 15 and 16.

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF** the Parties have executed this Agreement on the date set out at the head of this Agreement.

Signed for and on behalf of **Pemberton Engineering Ltd.**:

Name: Title: Date:

Signed for and on behalf of **[BIDDER NAME]**:

Name: Title: Date:

contents.text:
<!-- FILE: src/adeu/templates/markdown_ui.html -->
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>

<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Adeu Markdown UI</title>
    <meta name="color-scheme" content="light dark">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
    <link
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Jost:wght@400;500;600&family=Lora:ital,wght@0,600;1,600&display=swap"
        rel="stylesheet">
    <style>
        :root {
            --brand-sand: #e8c0a1;
            --brand-sand-light: #f2d9c7;
            --brand-sand-dark: #d4a882;
            --brand-mint: #e6f3f0;
            --brand-mint-dark: #c9e4de;
            --brand-navy: #1a2a2c;
            --brand-navy-light: #2a3a3c;
            --brand-mauve: #dacad2;
            --neutral-50: #fafafa;
            --neutral-100: #f5f5f5;
            --neutral-200: #e5e5e5;
            --neutral-300: #d9d9d9;
            --neutral-600: #555969;
            --neutral-800: #1e2028;
            --neutral-900: #171717;
            --font-lora: 'Lora', Georgia, serif;
            --font-jost: 'Jost', system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif;
            --bg-body: var(--neutral-50);
            --bg-card: #ffffff;
            --border-card: var(--neutral-200);
            --text-main: var(--neutral-600);
            --text-heading: var(--brand-navy);
            --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.15);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            :root {
                --bg-body: var(--neutral-900);
                --bg-card: var(--brand-navy);
                --border-card: var(--brand-navy-light);
                --text-main: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85);
                --text-heading: #ffffff;
                --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.1);
                --neutral-100: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        html,
        body {
            margin: 0;
            padding: 0;
            box-sizing: border-box;
            background-color: transparent;
        }

        body {
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            color: var(--text-main);
            line-height: 1.6;
            -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
            padding: 16px;
        }

        .adeu-card {
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            border: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 12px;
            overflow: hidden;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
        }

        .navbar {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            padding: 16px 32px;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: space-between;
            position: relative;
        }

        .logo-container {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            flex: 1;
        }

        .nav-title {
            position: absolute;
            left: 50%;
            transform: translateX(-50%);
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-size: 1.1rem;
            white-space: nowrap;
            overflow: hidden;
            text-overflow: ellipsis;
            max-width: 40%;
            text-align: center;
        }

        .nav-actions {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: flex-end;
            flex: 1;
        }

        #copy-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #copy-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        #open-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #open-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #copy-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
            #open-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        .logo-container svg {
            max-width: 80px;
            height: auto;
        }

        .logo-container svg path,
        .logo-container svg rect,
        .logo-container svg polygon,
        .logo-container svg circle {
            transition: fill 0.2s ease;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {

            .logo-container svg path,
            .logo-container svg rect,
            .logo-container svg polygon,
            .logo-container svg circle,
            .logo-container svg {
                fill: #ffffff !important;
            }
        }

        .content-wrapper {
            position: relative;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar {
            width: 6px;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
            background: transparent;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
            background-color: var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        #app {
            padding: 32px;
            display: flow-root;
        }

        .fade-overlay {
            position: absolute;
            bottom: 0;
            left: 0;
            right: 0;
            height: 80px;
            background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(255, 255, 255, 0), var(--bg-card));
            pointer-events: none;
            display: none;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .fade-overlay {
                background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(26, 42, 44, 0), var(--bg-card));
            }
        }

        .toggle-container {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            display: none;
            justify-content: center;
            align-items: center;
            padding: 12px;
            border-top: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
        }

        #toggle-btn {
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 0.95rem;
            cursor: pointer;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            padding: 8px 16px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #toggle-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #toggle-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        #toggle-icon {
            transition: transform 0.3s ease;
        }

        h1,
        h2,
        h3,
        h4 {
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-weight: 600;
            letter-spacing: -0.02em;
            margin-top: 1.5em;
            margin-bottom: 0.5em;
        }

        h1 {
            font-size: 2.25rem;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            padding-bottom: 0.3em;
            margin-top: 0;
        }

        h2 {
            font-size: 1.75rem;
        }

        h3 {
            font-size: 1.25rem;
        }

        a {
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            text-decoration: none;
            border-bottom: 1px solid transparent;
            transition: border 0.2s ease;
        }

        a:hover {
            border-bottom-color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
        }

        strong {
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
        }

        ul,
        ol {
            padding-left: 1.5rem;
            margin-bottom: 1rem;
        }

        li {
            margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
        }

        blockquote {
            border-left: 4px solid var(--brand-sand);
            padding: 12px 20px;
            margin: 24px 0;
            color: var(--text-main);
            background: var(--bg-blockquote);
            border-radius: 0 8px 8px 0;
            font-style: italic;
        }

        code {
            font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", Menlo, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            background: var(--neutral-100);
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            padding: 0.2em 0.4em;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        pre code {
            display: block;
            padding: 16px;
            background: var(--neutral-900);
            color: #e5e5e5;
            border-radius: 8px;
            overflow-x: auto;
        }

        .skeleton {
            background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--neutral-100) 25%, var(--border-card) 50%, var(--neutral-100) 75%);
            background-size: 200% 100%;
            animation: skeleton 1.5s ease-in-out infinite;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        @keyframes skeleton {
            0% {
                background-position: 200% 0;
            }

            100% {
                background-position: -200% 0;
            }
        }

        .alert {
            padding: 16px;
            border-radius: 8px;
            margin-bottom: 20px;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
            gap: 8px;
            background-color: #fef2f2;
            border: 1px solid #fecaca;
            color: #991b1b;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .alert {
                background-color: rgba(127, 29, 29, 0.2);
                border-color: rgba(248, 113, 113, 0.3);
                color: #fca5a5;
            }
        }

        .alert-title {
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 1.1em;
            margin: 0;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 8px;
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
        }

        .alert-body {
            margin: 0;
            font-size: 0.95em;
        }

        .alert-details {
            background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);
            padding: 12px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            font-family: ui-monospace, Consolas, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            white-space: pre-wrap;
            word-break: break-all;
            margin-top: 8px;
            border: 1px solid rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
        }
    </style>

            <div class="logo-container">
